Appendix: The Studies We Are Aware Of
I. That two people genuinely present with each other show measurable physiological alignment
II. That heart-rate variability indexes a regulated nervous system and tracks emotional and cognitive self-regulation
III. That the body's inner signals — interoception — shape emotion, decision, and self-knowledge ("listening to the within")
IV. That being accurately seen and understood is a basic human need with a measurable neural signature
V. That groups possess a measurable collective intelligence shaped by social sensitivity and turn-taking — a claim that is real but contested
VI. That nervous systems differ — so a measure of presence must be baselined to each person, never to a population norm
